title: Ilex longepedunculata
intro: Ilex longepedunculata, also known as the long-stalked holly, is a shrub or small tree species within the holly family (Aquifoliaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ilex longepedunculata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly prepared to promote digestive health, alleviate pain, and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Ilex longepedunculata is a species of holly, and its classification falls under the family Aquifoliaceae. This family is a part of the larger order Aquifoliales. Ilex longepedunculata belongs to the genus Ilex, which comprises a large group of holly species. The species is native to China and is often classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree. Its classification also includes being a member of the class Magnoliopsida and the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Ilex longepedunculata has been traditionally used in some parts of Asia for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. Its leaves and branches have been used in folk remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. More research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Ilex longepedunculata. Its traditional medicinal uses highlight the plant's potential as a natural remedy for various health conditions.
